description:
Balance life's Main and Side Quests in a private, offline game-themed to-do app
I built Quest Log to help myself, and maybe you, keep a balance between the two kinds of things a life is made of: the important ones it demands of us (Main Quests), and the beautiful ones that make it worth living (Side Quests). The app keeps both in view and helps you pick a few to focus on each day.

Every quest can hold objectives, a trail of progress, and a dated journal of what you did, so finishing one feels like finishing a quest rather than ticking a box. Sort quests into your own categories, mark the recurring ones as habits, and build each day from them.

It's yours to shape: switch between game-inspired themes, adjust the look, and tune a wide range of settings until the app feels right for you. The playful design is there to bring a little joy to getting things done.

It's private by default: no accounts, no network, no telemetry, and it works fully offline. Every quest is a plain Markdown file you can export, back up, and re-import as a ZIP, so your data is always yours.

Quest Log is young and changing fast, and your feedback shapes it.
